title: Fix colocate drift: HEAD silently flips to refs/jj/root mid-session
body:
# Goal

Make working in a colocated jj+git repo not silently
corrupt state. End state: running `jjf <verb>` from inside
this repo does NOT move git HEAD off `main`, does NOT
cause subsequent `git commit` to land on a phantom root,
and does NOT make `git status` show the whole repo as
"deleted." A pre/post hook, a guard, or a documented
isolation pattern — whichever is the right shape after
investigation.

# Why now

This footgun has bitten the orchestration loop twice in
one session (2026-06-22). Symptoms each time:

1. Run `jjf ls` or `jjf comment` from inside the jjforge
   repo.
2. jj moves the working copy `@` off `main` onto an empty
   descendant commit of the `bugs` bookmark.
3. Subsequent `git status` shows the whole tree (every
   file outside `crates/`, `experiments/`, `target/`) as
   deleted — git sees an empty tree against `main`.
4. Subsequent `git commit` lands on `refs/jj/root` (the
   jj-internals empty-root marker) as a root commit, not
   on top of `main`.
5. Recovery: `git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main` +
   `git reset --hard main`, which is destructive and
   blocked by default permissions, requiring user
   intervention.

The CLAUDE.md "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo"
section already documents `jj edit main` as the recovery
step, but that ALSO rebases the descendant commits, which
rewrote a previously-committed SHA in this round.

So we have two failure modes:
- Drift: `jjf` silently flips git HEAD.
- Recovery rewrites: `jj edit main` re-SHAs the chain.

The right fix is probably some combination of: a
preflight in the CLI that detects "running from inside
the jjforge repo" and refuses (or warns), a documented
isolation pattern (always run `jjf` from a scratch dir),
or a post-hook that restores git HEAD after each `jjf`
call.

# Scope

## Investigation

- **Reproduce reliably.** Identify the exact `jjf` verb
  sequence that triggers the drift. Suspect: any verb
  that does the 4-CLI write dance (`jj new bookmarks(bugs)`
  → edit working copy → describe → bookmark set). Read
  verbs (`show`, `ls`, `read`) shouldn't drift because
  they don't run `jj new` — verify.
- **Determine if it's jj 0.40-specific or general.** The
  4-CLI dance assumes jj version-pinned semantics. Check
  with `jj --version`.
- **Determine if `--repository <path>` would avoid it.**
  jjf's `Storage::*` shell out to `jj` from the cwd; if
  we shelled out with `--repository <repo_root>` and
  also used a sibling working-copy or `--ignore-working-copy`,
  maybe `@` doesn't have to move at all.

## Options to evaluate during investigation

A. **CLI preflight: refuse to write from inside the
   jjforge source repo.** A read of `jj root` + check if
   it matches a known "this is the jjforge repo itself"
   marker (presence of `crates/jjf/Cargo.toml` would do).
   Verbs that mutate (`new`, `update`, `comment`, ...)
   exit 2 with a clear message: "you're inside the
   jjforge source repo; use a sibling working dir or set
   JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1." Cheap, safe, possibly
   over-conservative.

B. **CLI `--ignore-working-copy` (or equivalent).**
   Switch the storage layer's `jj` invocations to use
   `--ignore-working-copy` where possible (`jj describe`,
   `jj bookmark set`), and create a separate jj
   workspace under `.jj-jjforge-workspace/` (or a
   temp-dir-per-call) for the write-dance steps that
   actually need a working copy. This is the
   "isolation" approach.

C. **Post-call HEAD restore hook.** Stash git HEAD
   before any `jj new` step, restore after. Probably the
   most fragile.

D. **Workspace pattern: run `jjf` from a sibling
   directory.** Document this as the canonical operator
   pattern: `cd ~/p/jjforge-data && jjf ls` against a
   sibling clone of the repo that has the bookmark but
   isn't the source tree. Cheapest, requires user
   discipline.

Recommended default after investigation: **A + D
combined** — the preflight is a safety net, the sibling
workspace is the doc'd pattern. B is a real engineering
investment that probably wants its own ticket.

## Code changes (if A is selected)

- `crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs` (new sibling fn or a
  new module).
- Detection logic: `jj root` returns the repo root; check
  for known markers (`crates/jjf/Cargo.toml`,
  `docs/storage-format.md`, `Cargo.toml` with
  `package.name = "jjforge"` workspace member).
- Bypass: `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1` env var.
- Apply to mutating verbs only: `new`, `update`,
  `comment`, `close`, `open`, `label add|rm`, `push`,
  `pull`, `remote add|rm`. Read verbs untouched.

## Docs

- `CLAUDE.md` "Operating in a colocated jj+git repo"
  section: update to reflect whichever path lands.
- `docs/cli-json.md`: new error kind
  `self_hosted_write_refused` (or similar) if option A.
- `README.md`: "Working on jjforge itself" subsection
  pointing operators at the sibling-workspace pattern.

# Acceptance

- [ ] Investigation findings recorded in the closing
  comment: which `jjf` verbs drift, which jj version,
  what `--ignore-working-copy` would buy.
- [ ] Option chosen (A / B / C / D / hybrid) with the
  rationale.
- [ ] The chosen guard / pattern is implemented and
  tested.
- [ ] Integration test: run a mutating verb from inside
  the jjforge source repo (in a scratch fixture that
  mirrors the marker shape); assert the chosen guard
  fires (or the chosen isolation works).
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- Switching to option B (full workspace isolation) if
  the investigation says A+D is sufficient. File a
  separate ticket if B becomes worthwhile.
- Fixing `jj edit main`'s SHA rewriting (jj-side
  concern; not our problem).
- General jj-vs-git colocate ergonomics — only the
  jjforge-specific drift.

# Pointers

- Existing CLAUDE.md guidance: "Operating in a
  colocated jj+git repo" section.
- Storage 4-CLI dance: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`
  search for `jj new bookmarks(bugs)`.
- Preflight infra: `crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs`.
- The two times this hit in 2026-06-22 are recoverable
  via the orchestration session log; no ticket
  references because the symptoms predate this
  ticket's existence.
